_meta.status: rough working draft — muscle registry. each muscle has a scientific name and an optional common name (null where there's no nickname). the catalog references the scientific name. values are lowercase; case/format for presentation as needed. regions = the broad buckets (lower back is in two).
_meta.granularity_note: Muscle granularity is uneven on purpose: neck and chest are specific (many muscles); quad/glutes stay coarse. A muscle may belong to more than one region (lower back = back + core).

muscles.note: front and side of the neck; flexes and rotates the head
side-front of the neck; assist neck flexion and lateral bend
back of the neck; extend the head and neck
from the upper neck to the shoulder blade; elevates the scapula
deep muscles at the base of the skull; fine head and neck extension
front of the shoulder; raises the arm forward
side of the shoulder; raises the arm out to the side
back of the shoulder; draws the arm backward
four deep muscles that stabilize and rotate the shoulder joint
upper chest, along the collarbone
mid and lower chest, along the breastbone
small muscle beneath the pectoralis major, on the ribs
broad muscle of the mid and lower back; adducts and extends the arm
elevates the shoulder blade
retracts the shoulder blade
depresses the shoulder blade
between the shoulder blades; retract the scapula
from the shoulder blade to the upper arm; adducts and internally rotates the arm
columns of muscle running along the spine; extend and brace the trunk; sits in both back and core
front of the upper arm; flexes the elbow and supinates the forearm
back of the upper arm; extends the elbow
beneath the biceps; a primary elbow flexor
forearm muscle crossing the elbow; flexes the elbow
front of the forearm; flex the wrist and fingers (grip)
back of the forearm; extend the wrist
pronators and supinators; rotate the forearm
the six-pack; flexes the trunk
sides of the trunk; rotate and side-bend the torso
deepest abdominal layer; braces the trunk
on the ribcage beneath the arm; protracts the shoulder blade and ties into the obliques
front of the thigh; extends the knee
back of the thigh; bend the knee and extend the hip
largest hip muscle; the main hip extensor
side of the hip; abduct the thigh and stabilize the pelvis
inner thigh; pull the leg toward the midline
deep hip flexors; raise the thigh and flex the trunk
upper calf; the visible diamond-shaped muscle
deeper calf, beneath the gastrocnemius
front of the shin; lifts the foot
